title: API Gateway Technology Selection
context: The Cloud Modernisation Programme requires a managed API gateway to expose platform microservices to internal consumers and external partners. The gateway must support mTLS, rate limiting, and OAuth2/OIDC token validation without requiring custom middleware on every service. Architecture principle §3.1 mandates that all external traffic terminates at a managed edge layer.
decision: We will use a managed cloud-native API gateway with support for OAuth2/OIDC, mTLS termination, and GitOps-managed declarative routing policies.
rationale: The managed gateway is the only option that satisfies all three architecture principle constraints (§3.1, §3.2, §5) without requiring a dedicated gateway operations team. Self-managed options were considered but rejected due to high operational burden and failure to meet the policy-as-code requirement.

alternatives.reason_rejected: High operational burden; does not meet policy-as-code requirement (architecture-principles.md §3.2)
Adds complexity without additional capability at current scale

positive: Centralised policy enforcement reduces per-service boilerplate
Managed SLA removes the need for a dedicated gateway operations team
negative: Vendor lock-in for gateway-specific routing features
Cold-start latency on auto-scaling may affect P99 response times under burst load
implementation_tasks: Configure mTLS between gateway and backend services
Implement OAuth2 token validation at the gateway layer
Define rate limiting policies per client tier
Set up GitOps pipeline for gateway configuration
Document failover runbook for DR activation
